advantages: One model family covers writing, analysis, and coding—reduces context switching between tools.
Multimodal and tool-use paths scale from chat experiments to production agents.
Strong instruction-following for format constraints (tables, JSON-like structures) when prompted clearly.
Extended thinking modes (where available) improve reliability on complex single-shot problems.
disadvantages: Capability varies sharply by model tier—misconfiguration wastes time or money.
Tool-use and vision add integration and security work beyond simple chat.
Generated code and analysis can be subtly wrong—human verification remains mandatory.
Latency and token costs rise with long contexts, images, and multi-step tool loops.
